headline: Terengganu Oil Royalty Dispute Resurfaces With RM8.2B Claim
context: Terengganu filed a revised claim for RM8.2 billion in oil royalties allegedly underpaid by the federal government since 2000, arguing that Petronas calculations systematically undervalued production from the state's offshore fields.

cards.big: RM8.2 billion in allegedly unpaid oil royalties — Terengganu claims Petronas systematically undercounted production for 25 years
cards.sub: State's own petroleum engineers estimate 18% higher production volumes than Petronas official figures

cards.big: Terengganu receives RM1.8 billion annual royalty but claims RM2.4 billion is owed based on independent production audit
cards.sub: Cumulative underpayment claim of RM8.2 billion would fund 25 years of state development backlog

cards.big: Terengganu's poverty rate at 8.4% is 2.5x national average despite state being 3rd largest petroleum producer
cards.sub: Kuala Terengganu's infrastructure quality ranked 11th of 13 state capitals — oil wealth hasn't translated to development

cards.big: Petroleum royalty calculation methodology is classified — states cannot independently verify production volumes or revenue figures
cards.sub: Petronas audits its own production data with no state government access to wellhead meters or export documentation

cards.big: When the company extracting your resources also controls the meter and the calculator, the state never knows what it's actually owed
Should petroleum-producing states have the right to install independent production monitors at all wellheads in their waters?
